Student, University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign

2023 — present

At the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign, from August 2023 to May 2025(Expected), I am pursuing a Master of Science in Information Management. My studies include relevant coursework in Data Visualization, Information Modeling, and Data Statistical Models, equipping me with advanced knowledge and practical skills pertinent to the information field.

Student, Sungkyunkwan University

2016 — 2022

I completed a dual degree program at Sungkyunkwan University in South Korea, earning a Bachelor of Science in Computer Education and a Bachelor of Arts in Education. During my studies, I had the opportunity to participate in an exchange student program, attending the University of Copenhagen in Denmark and Sheffield University in the United Kingdom. I was also the recipient of the Studying Abroad Scholarship and won the Smart Car Arduino Design Contest.

Online Platform Python Instructor, Coding Valley

2023 — 2023

I developed and taught Python courses emphasizing real-world applications, resulting in higher student enrollment.

Data Analyst, OneAll&Partners

2021 — 2022

I spearheaded a research initiative on blockchain trends in partnership with the Singapore headquarters, pinpointing crucial investment sectors. My analysis of the most recent developments in blockchain markets, particularly in decentralized finance (De-Fi) and non-fungible tokens (NFTs), provided strategic business insights that contributed to a 15% rise in knowledgeable investment decisions. Furthermore, I interpreted blockchain transactional data, underscoring significant factors for cybersecurity and data integrity, ensuring that these essential aspects were given due consideration in our strategic approach.

Teaching Assistant, Sungkyunkwan University

2021 — 2021

As a teaching assistant for the "GEDT020 Foundation and Application of Artificial Intelligence" course, I conducted weekly tutorial sessions to bolster student comprehension of intricate AI concepts. My role extended to serving as a crucial intermediary between students and faculty, where I communicated feedback and concerns with the aim of refining and optimizing the learning experience.

Research Assistant, Sungkyunkwan University

2021 — 2020

In my research, I conducted experiments using C and C++ to explore "Tile-based Viewport-Adaptive Streaming for Immersive Media," utilizing techniques such as user viewpoint-based tile segmentation, transmission methods, and Region Wise Packing (RWP). My focused attention to detail was instrumental in enhancing latency and bandwidth performance, resulting in a significant 30% improvement in 360-degree video streaming.
